title: Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp
description: Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: "A Border Affair"
memes.description: "Spanish is the lovin’ tongue, Soft as music, light as spray; ’Twas a girl I learnt it from Livin’ down Sonora way. I don’t look much like a lover, Yet I say her love-words over Often, when I’m all alo…"

memes.label: "A Cowboy Alone with His Conscience"
memes.description: "When I ride into the mountains on my little broncho bird, Whar my ears are never pelted with the bawlin’ o’ the herd, An’ a sort o’ dreamy quiet hangs upon the western air, An’ thar ain’t no animation…"

memes.label: "A Cowboy Race"
memes.description: "A pattering rush like the rattle of hail When the storm king’s wild coursers are out on the trail, A long roll of hoofs—and the earth is a drum! The centaurs! See! Over the prairies they come! A rolli…"

memes.label: "A Cowboy Song"
memes.description: "I could not be so well content, So sure of thee, Señorita, But well I know you must relent And come to me, Lolita! The Caballeros throng to see Thy laughing face, Señorita, Lolita. But well I know thy…"

memes.label: "A Cowboy Toast"
memes.description: "Here’s to the passing cowboy, the plowman’s pioneer; His home, the boundless [[mesa]], he of any man the peer; Around his wide [[sombrero]] was stretched the rattler’s hide, His bridle sporting conchos, his l…"

memes.label: "A Cowboy at the Carnival"
memes.description: "Yes, o’ cose it’s interestin’ to a feller from the range, Mighty queerish, too, I tell you—sich a racket fer a change; From a life among the cattle, from a wool shirt and the chaps To the biled shirt …"

memes.label: "A Cowboy’s Hopeless Love"
memes.description: "I’ve heard that story ofttimes about that little chap A-cryin’ for the shiney moon to fall into his lap, An’ jes a-raisin’ merry hell because he couldn’t git The same to swing down low so’s he could n…"

memes.label: "A Cowboy’s Love Song"
memes.description: "Oh, the last steer has been branded And the last beef has been shipped, And I’m free to roam the prairies That the round-up crew has stripped; I’m free to think of Susie— Fairer than the stars above—"

memes.label: "A Cowboy’s Son"
memes.description: "Whar y’u from, little stranger, little boy? Y’u was ridin’ a cloud on that star-strewn plain, But y’u fell from the skies like a drop of rain To this world of sorrow and long, long pain. Will y’u care…"

memes.label: "A Cowboy’s Worrying Love"
memes.description: "I ust to read in the novel books ’bout fellers that got the prod From an arrer shot from his hidin’ place by the hand o’ the Cupid god, An’ I’d laugh at the cussed chumps they was a-wastin’ their brea…"

memes.label: "A Dance at the Ranch"
memes.description: "From every point they gaily come, the broncho’s unshod feet Pat at the green sod of the range with quick, emphatic beat; The tresses of the buxom girls as banners stream behind— Like silken, castigati…"

memes.label: "A Nevada Cowpuncher to His Beloved"
memes.description: "Lonesome? Well, I guess so! This place is mighty blue; The silence of the empty rooms Jes’ palpitates with—you. The day has lost its beauty, The sun’s a-shinin’ pale; I’ll round up my belongin’s An’ I…"

memes.label: "A Ranger"
memes.description: "He never made parade of tooth or claw; He was plain as us that nursed the bawlin’ herds. Though he had a rather meanin’-lookin’ jaw, He was shy of exercisin’ it with words. As a circus-ridin’ preacher…"

memes.label: Abilene
memes.description: "Redirect to [[abilene]], the Kansas cowtown at the terminus of the Chisholm Trail."

memes.label: Adventure

memes.label: "At a Cowboy Dance"
memes.description: "Git yo’ little sagehens ready; Trot ’em out upon the floor— Line up there, you critters! Steady! Lively, now! One couple more. Shorty, shed that ol’ sombrero; Broncho, douse that cigaret; Stop yer cus…"

memes.label: "Bar-Z on a Sunday Night"
memes.description: "We ain’t no saints on the Bar-Z ranch, ’Tis said—an’ we know who ’tis— “Th’ devil’s laid hold on us, tooth an’ branch, An’ uses us in his biz.” Still, we ain’t so bad but we might be wuss, An’ you’d s…"

memes.label: "Broncho Versus Bicycle"
memes.description: "The first that we saw of the high-tone tramp War over thar at our Pecos camp; He war comin’ down the Santa Fe trail Astride of a wheel with a crooked tail, A-skinnin’ along with a merry song An’ a-rin…"

memes.label: "Cowboy Songs and Other Frontier Ballads"
memes.description: "John A. Lomax's earlier and larger collection of cowboy songs and frontier ballads, the precursor to the present anthology. (P03-002)"

memes.label: "Cowboy Versus Broncho"
memes.description: "Haven’t got no special likin’ fur the toney sorts o’ play, Chasin’ foxes or that hossback polo game, Jumpin’ critters over hurdles—sort o’ things that any jay Could accomplish an’ regard as rather tam…"

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "In affectionate gratitude to a group of men, my intimate friends during College days (brought under one roof by a “Fraternity”), whom I still love not less but more, - Will Prather, Hammett Hardy, Pen…"

memes.label: "Denver Jim"
memes.description: "“Say, fellers, that ornery thief must be nigh us, For I jist saw him across this way to the right; Ah, there he is now right under that burr-oak As fearless and cool as if waitin’ all night. Well, com…"

memes.label: "Epigraph"
memes.description: "Book epigraph poem evoking the fading frontier — cowboys turned from rifles to songs, a saga of the strong (P02-001)."

memes.label: "High Chin Bob"
memes.description: "’Way high up in the Mokiones, among the mountain tops, A lion cleaned a yearling’s bones and licks his thankful chops; And who upon the scene should ride, a-trippin’ down the slope, But High Chin Bob …"

memes.label: "I Want My Time"
memes.description: "I’m night guard all alone tonight, Dead homesick, lonely, tired and blue; And none but you can make it right; My heart is hungry, Girl, for you. I’ve longed all night to hug you, Dear; To speak my lov…"

memes.label: "Introduction"
memes.description: "Introduction The Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp does not purport to be an anthology of Western verse. As its title indicates, the contents of the book are limited to attempts, more or less po…"

memes.label: "Jack Dempsey’s Grave"
memes.description: "Far out in the wilds of Oregon, On a lonely mountain side, Where Columbia’s mighty waters Roll down to the Ocean’s tide; Where the giant fir and cedar Are imaged in the wave, O’ergrown with ferns and …"

memes.label: "Just A-Ridin’!"
memes.description: "Oh, for me a horse and saddle Every day without a change; With the desert sun a-blazin’ On a hundred miles o’ range, Just a-ridin’, just a-ridin’, Desert ripplin’ in the sun, Mountains blue along the …"

memes.label: "Lasca"
memes.description: "I want free life, and I want fresh air; And I sigh for the canter after the cattle, The crack of the whips like shots in battle, The medley of hoofs and horns and heads That wars and wrangles and scat…"

memes.label: "Love Lyrics of a Cowboy"
memes.description: "It hain’t no use fer me to say There’s others with a style an’ way That beats hers to a fare-you-well, Fer, on the square, I’m here to tell I jes can’t even start to see But what she’s perfect as kin …"

memes.label: "Marta of Milrone"
memes.description: "I shot him where the Rio flows; I shot him when the moon arose; And where he lies the vulture knows Along the Tinto River. In schools of eastern culture pale My cloistered flesh began to fail; They bo…"

memes.label: "Our Little Cowgirl"
memes.description: "Thar she goes a-lopin’, stranger, Khaki-gowned, with flyin’ hair, Talk about your classy ridin’— Wal, you’re gettin’ it right thar. Jest a kid, but lemme tell you When she warms a saddle seat On that …"

memes.label: "Out Where the West Begins"
memes.description: "Out where the handclasp’s a little stronger, Out where the smile dwells a little longer, That’s where the West begins; Out where the sun is a little brighter, Where the snows that fall are a trifle wh…"

memes.label: "Pardners"
memes.description: "You bad-eyed, tough-mouthed son-of-a-gun, Ye’re a hard little beast to break, But ye’re good for the fiercest kind of a run An’ ye’re quick as a rattlesnake. Ye jolted me good when we first met In the…"

memes.label: "Riders of the Stars"
memes.description: "Twenty abreast down the Golden Street ten thousand riders marched; Bow-legged boys in their swinging chaps, all clumsily keeping time; And the Angel Host to the lone, last ghost their delicate eyebrow…"

memes.label: "Ridin’ Up the Rocky Trail from Town"
memes.description: "We’re the children of the open and we hate the haunts o’ men, But we had to come to town to get the mail. And we’re ridin’ home at daybreak—’cause the air is cooler then— All ’cept one of us that stop…"

memes.label: "Riding Song"
memes.description: "Let us ride together— Blowing mane and hair, Careless of the weather, Miles ahead of care, Ring of hoof and snaffle, Swing of waist and hip, Trotting down the twisted road With the world let slip. Let…"

memes.label: "Snagtooth Sal"
memes.description: "I was young and happy and my heart was light and gay, Singin’, always singin’ through the sunny summer day; Happy as a lizard in the wavin’ chaparral, Walkin’ down through Laramie with Snagtooth Sal."

memes.label: "Song of the Cattle Trail"
memes.description: "The dust hangs thick upon the trail And the horns and the hoofs are clashing, While off at the side through the chaparral The men and the strays go crashing; But in right good cheer the cowboy sings,"

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "The Bandit’s Grave"
memes.description: "’Mid lava rock and glaring sand, ’Neath the desert’s brassy skies, Bound in the silent chains of death A border bandit lies. The poppy waves her golden glow Above the lowly mound; The cactus stands wi…"

memes.label: "The Bronc That Wouldn’t Bust"
memes.description: "I’ve busted bronchos off and on Since first I struck their trail, And you bet I savvy bronchos From nostrils down to tail; But I struck one on Powder River, And say, hands, he was the first And only l…"

memes.label: "The Bull Fight"
memes.description: "The couriers from Chihuahua go To distant Cusi and Santavo, Announce the feast of all the year the crown— Se corren los toros! And Juan brings his Pepita into town. The rancherias on the mountain side…"

memes.label: "The Bunkhouse Orchestra"
memes.description: "Wrangle up your mouth-harps, drag your banjo out, Tune your old guitarra till she twangs right stout, For the snow is on the mountains and the wind is on the plain, But we’ll cut the chimney’s moanin’…"

memes.label: "The Call of the Plains"
memes.description: "Ho! wind of the far, far prairies! Free as the waves of the sea! Your voice is sweet as in alien street The cry of a friend to me! You bring me the breath of the prairies, Known in the days that are s…"

memes.label: "The Cattle Round-Up"
memes.description: "Once more are we met for a season of pleasure, That shall smooth from our brows every furrow of care, For the sake of old times shall we each tread a measure And drink to the lees in the eyes of the f…"

memes.label: "The Chase"
memes.description: "Here’s a moccasin track in the drifts, It’s no more than the length of my hand; An’ her instep—just see how it lifts! If that ain’t the best in the land! For the maid ran as free as the wind And her f…"

memes.label: "The Clown’s Baby"
memes.description: "It was on the western frontier— The miners, rugged and brown, Were gathered round the posters, The circus had come to town! The great tent shone in the darkness Like a wonderful palace of light, And r…"

memes.label: "The Cowboy"
memes.description: "He wears a big hat and big spurs and all that, And leggins of fancy fringed leather; He takes pride in his boots and the pistol he shoots, And he’s happy in all kinds of weather; He’s fond of his hors…"

memes.label: "The Cowboy and the Maid"
memes.description: "Funny how it come about! Me and Texas Tom was out Takin’ of a moonlight walk, Fillin’ in the time with talk. Every star up in the sky Seemed to wink the other eye At each other, ’sif they Smelt a mous…"

memes.label: "The Cowboy to His Friend in Need"
memes.description: "You’re very well polished, I’m free to confess, Well balanced, well rounded, a power for right; But cool and collected—no steel could be less; You’re primed for continual fight. Your voice is a bellic…"

memes.label: "The Cowboys’ Ball"
memes.description: "Yip! Yip! Yip! Yip! tunin’ up the fiddle; You an’ take yo’r pardner there, standin’ by the wall! Say “How!” make a bow, and sashay down the middle; Shake yo’r leg lively at the Cowboys’ Ball. Big feet…"

memes.label: "The Cowboys’ Christmas Ball"
memes.description: "Way out in Western Texas, where the Clear Fork’s waters flow, Where the cattle are “a-browzin’ ” and the Spanish ponies grow; Where the Norther “comes a-whistlin’ ” from beyond the Neutral strip And t…"

memes.label: "The Cowboy’s Dance Song"
memes.description: "You can’t expect a cowboy to agitate his shanks In etiquettish manner in aristocratic ranks When he’s always been accustomed to shake the heel and toe At the rattling rancher dances where much etiquet…"

memes.label: "The Cowboy’s Valentine"
memes.description: "Say, Moll, now don’t you ’llow to quit A-playin’ maverick? Sech stock should be corralled a bit An’ hev a mark ’t ’ll stick. Old Val’s a-roundin’-up today Upon the Sweetheart Range, ’N me a-helpin’, s…"

memes.label: "The Dance at Silver Valley"
memes.description: "Don’t you hear the big spurs jingle? Don’t you feel the red blood tingle? Be it smile or be it frown, Be it dance or be it fight, Broncho Bill has come to town To dance a dance tonight. Chaps, sombrer…"

memes.label: "The Desert"
memes.description: "’Twas the lean coyote told me, baring his slavish soul, As I counted the ribs of my dead cayuse and cursed at the desert sky, The tale of the Upland Rider’s fate while I dug in the water hole For a dr…"

memes.label: "The Disappointed Tenderfoot"
memes.description: "He reached the West in a palace car where the writers tell us the cowboys are, With the redskin bold and the centipede and the rattlesnake and the loco weed. He looked around for the Buckskin Joes and…"

memes.label: "The Drunken Desperado"
memes.description: "I’m wild and woolly and full of fleas, I’m hard to curry below the knees, I’m a she-wolf from Shamon Creek, For I was dropped from a lightning streak And it’s my night to hollow—Whoo‑pee! I stayed in …"

memes.label: "The End of the Trail"
memes.description: "Soh, Bossie, soh! The water’s handy heah, The grass is plenty neah, An’ all the stars a-sparkle Bekaze we drive no mo’— We drive no mo’. The long trail ends today— The long trail ends today, The punch…"

memes.label: "The Gila Monster Route"
memes.description: "The lingering sunset across the plain Kissed the rear-end door of an east-bound train, And shone on a passing track close by Where a ding-bat sat on a rotting tie. He was ditched by a shock and a crue…"

memes.label: "The Glory Trail"
memes.description: "’Way high up the Mogollons,1 Among the mountain tops, A lion cleaned a yearlin’s bones And licked his thankful chops, When on the picture who should ride, A-trippin’ down the slope, But High-Chin Bob,…"

memes.label: "The Habit"
memes.description: "I’ve beat my way wherever any winds have blown; I’ve bummed along from Portland down to San Antone; From Sandy Hook to Frisco, over gulch and hill— For once you git the habit, why, you can’t keep stil…"

memes.label: "The Insult"
memes.description: "I’ve swum the Colorado where she runs close down to hell; I’ve braced the faro layouts in Cheyenne; I’ve fought for muddy water with a bunch of howlin’ swine An’ swallowed hot tamales and cayenne; I’v…"

memes.label: "The Land of the Bitterroot"
memes.description: "A poem celebrating the Bitterroot Valley — 'The Indian land—Land of the Bitterroot.' (072-001)"

memes.label: "The Legend of Boastful Bill"
memes.description: "At a round-up on the Gila One sweet morning long ago, Ten of us was throwed quite freely By a hoss from Idaho. An’ we ’lowed he’d go a-beggin’ For a man to break his pride Till, a-hitchin’ up one legg…"

memes.label: "The Ol’ Cow Hawse"
memes.description: "When it comes to saddle hawses, there’s a difference in steeds: There is fancy-gaited critters that will suit some feller’s needs; There is nags high-bred an’ tony, with a smooth an’ shiny skin, That …"

memes.label: "The Old Chisholm Trail"
memes.description: "The historic cattle trail referenced in the anthology's poems — 'the old Chisholm Trail' when 'the world was all new.' (019-001)"

memes.label: "The Old Cowman"
memes.description: "I rode across a valley range I hadn’t seen for years. The trail was all so spoilt and strange It nearly fetched the tears. I had to let ten fences down— (The fussy lanes ran wrong) And each new line w…"

memes.label: "The Old Mackenzie Trail"
memes.description: "See, stretching yonder o’er that low divide Which parts the falling rain—the eastern slope Sends down its waters to the southern sea Through Double Mountain’s winding length of stream; The western sid…"

memes.label: "The Outlaw"
memes.description: "When my loop takes hold on a two-year-old, By the feet or the neck or the horn, He kin plunge and fight till his eyes go white, But I’ll throw him as sure as you’re born. Though the taut rope sing lik…"

memes.label: "“The Road to Ruin”"
memes.description: "“The Road to Ruin”2 I went into the grog-shop, Tom, and stood beside the bar, And drank a glass of lemonade and smoked a bad seegar. The same old kegs and jugs was thar, the same we used to know When …"

memes.label: "The Shallows of the Ford"
memes.description: "Did you ever wait for daylight when the stars along the river Floated thick and white as snowflakes in the water deep and strange, Till a whisper through the aspens made the current break and shiver A…"

memes.label: "The Sheep-Herder"
memes.description: "The Sheep-Herder3 All day across the sagebrush flat, Beneath the sun of June, My sheep they loaf and feed and bleat Their never changin’ tune. And then, at night time, when they lay As quiet as a ston…"

memes.label: "The Texas Cowboy and the Mexican Greaser"
memes.description: "I think we can all remember when a Greaser hadn’t no show In Palo Pinto particular—it ain’t very long ago; A powerful feelin’ of hatred ag’in the whole Greaser race That murdered bold Crockett and Bow…"

memes.label: "The Transformation of a Texas Girl"
memes.description: "She was a Texas maiden, she came of low degree, Her clothes were worn and faded, her feet from shoes were free; Her face was tanned and freckled, her hair was sun-burned, too, Her whole darned tout en…"

memes.label: "The Vigilantes"
memes.description: "We are the whirlwinds that winnow the West— We scatter the wicked like straw! We are the Nemeses, never at rest— We are Justice, and Right, and the Law! Moon on the snow and a blood-chilling blast, Sh…"

memes.label: "To Hear Him Tell It"
memes.description: "I was just about to take a drink— I was mighty dry— So I hailed an old time cowman Who was passing by, “Come in, Ole Timer! have a drink! Kinda warm today!” As we leaned across the bar-rail— “How’s th…"

memes.label: "When Bob Got Throwed"
memes.description: "That time when Bob got throwed I thought I sure would bust. I like to died a-laffin’ To see him chewin’ dust. He crawled on that Andy bronc And hit him with a quirt. The next thing that he knew He was…"

memes.label: "When You’re Throwed"
memes.description: "If a feller’s been a-straddle Since he’s big enough to ride, And has had to sling his saddle On most any colored hide— Though it’s nothin’ they take pride in, Still most fellers I have knowed, If they…"

memes.label: "Where the Grizzly Dwells"
memes.description: "Where the Grizzly Dwells4 I admire the artificial art of the East; But I love more the inimitable art of the West, Where nature’s handiwork lies in virginal beauty. Amidst the hum of city life I saunt…"

memes.label: "Whiskey Bill—A Fragment"
memes.description: "Whiskey Bill—A Fragment A-down the road and gun in hand Comes Whiskey Bill, mad Whiskey Bill; A-lookin’ for some place to land Comes Whiskey Bill. An’ everybody’d like to be Ten miles away behind a tr…"

memes.label: "Who’s That Calling So Sweet?"
memes.description: "The herds are gathered in from plain and hill, Who’s that a-calling? The boys are sleeping and the boys are still, Who’s that a-calling? ’Twas the wind a-sighing in the prairie grass, Who’s that a-cal…"

memes.label: "A Border Affair (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of 'A Border Affair' by Charles Badger Clark — the anthology's most famous bilingual love poem, told in a mix of English and Spanish that captures the cultural borderlands of the American Southwest."

memes.label: "A Cowboy Alone with His Conscience (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the introspective poem exploring guilt, solitude, and moral reckoning on the empty prairie—the cowboy's internal drama."

memes.label: "A Cowboy Race (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the lively poem depicting a cowboy horse race—competition, pride, and the cowboy's love of speed and daring on the open range."

memes.label: "A Cowboy Song (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the lyric poem celebrating the cowboy's life of freedom, song, and the open sky—a pure expression of Western joy."

memes.label: "A Cowboy Toast (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of Adams's celebratory toast to cowboy life—a ritual of camaraderie, gratitude, and the pleasures of the trail shared around the campfire."

memes.label: "Abilene"
memes.description: "Abilene as a cattle trail town—one of the great railheads that received Texas herds and the fine goods that came west by stage. A waypoint on the Mackenzie Trail route across Texas."

memes.label: "Adventure"
memes.description: "The theme of adventure and the open road in cattle trail poetry—the cowboy's embrace of danger, the unknown, and the thrill of the long drive."

memes.label: "The Alamo"
memes.description: "The Alamo appears in \"Lasca\" as the San Antonio landmark where the narrator encounters the passionate and dangerous heroine, linking the historic mission to a tale of love and violence on the Texas frontier."

memes.label: "Animal Imagery"
memes.description: "The rich animal imagery of the cattle trail poems—horses, cattle, coyotes, wolves, and the creatures that share the cowboy's vast landscape."

memes.label: "Arizona Territory"
memes.description: "Arizona Territory in the era of the cattle trail—a land of desert, canyon, and range that features in the geography of Western cowboy verse."

memes.label: "Arkansas"
memes.description: "Arkansas, specifically Long Point, appears as the home of a waiting mother in \"[[denver-jim]]\"—the fixed point of settled life against which the vast, dangerous West is measured. A geography of separation and reunion in [[the-cattle-trail]] poems."

memes.label: Arthur Chapman
memes.description: "American cowboy poet, author of \"Out Where the West Begins\" (1916), the best-known poem in John A. Lomax's anthology *Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp*."

memes.label: "Austin"
memes.description: "Austin, Texas, appears in the Introduction to the anthology as the location of the University of Texas where editor John A. Lomax signed his preface, anchoring the collection in the intellectual life of the Lone Star State."

memes.label: Belle McClure
memes.description: "Fictional character, the woman at the center of the fatal love triangle in William Maxwell's narrative poem \"The Dance at Silver Valley\" in John A. Lomax's *Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp* (1916)."

memes.label: Berton Braley
memes.description: "American poet and journalist (1882–1966) who contributed two poems to John A. Lomax's anthology, including the cowboy-partnership ballad \"Pardners.\""

memes.label: "Bill"
memes.description: "Bill—a recurring named cowboy figure in the anthology whose adventures with broncos, bicycles, and bar fights represent the everyday humor and resilience of the trail hand."

memes.label: Billy Leamont
memes.description: "Title character of \"The Ballad of Billy Leamont,\" a tragic cowboy ballad fragment preserved in Chapter 74. Billy Leamont rides out of town with his companion Jack Lorell, discovers Lorell dead, and takes his own life — both riding 'through the streets of hell' together. (074-001)"

relations.label: Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp

memes.label: "Bitterroot Valley"
memes.description: "The Bitterroot Valley of Montana—celebrated in 'The Land of the Bitterroot' as a cowboy paradise of lush grass, clear water, and natural beauty."

memes.label: "Branding"
memes.description: "The practice of marking cattle with hot irons—a ritual of ownership, a symbol of ranch identity, and a recurring motif in cowboy verse."

memes.label: Broncho Bill
memes.description: "Fictional cowboy, the tragic hero of William Maxwell's narrative poem \"The Dance at Silver Valley\" in John A. Lomax's *Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp* (1916)."

memes.label: "Broncho Versus Bicycle (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the humorous encounter between cowboy tradition (the bronco) and modernity (the bicycle)—a comic meditation on change in the West."

memes.label: "Bronco Busting"
memes.description: "The art and danger of breaking wild horses—one of the cowboy's most iconic and perilous skills, celebrated in poems like 'Broncho Versus Bicycle' and 'Cowboy Versus Broncho.'"

memes.label: "Buckaroo"
memes.description: "A cowboy or bronco rider—an Americanization of the Spanish vaquero—appearing in cattle-trail verse as both the observer of skilled riding and the rider himself atop a bucking horse. (032-001)(042-001)"

memes.label: Burke Jenkins
memes.description: "Poet who contributed 'The Cowboy to His Friend in Need' (ch039) to the Lomax anthology, a poem expressing a cowboy's deep affection for his revolver."

memes.label: "Campfire Imagery"
memes.description: "The campfire as the cowboy's hearth on the open [[prairie]]—a center of warmth, story, song, and the temporary community of the trail crew."

memes.label: "Canyon"
memes.description: "A deep gorge carved by water through rock—a landscape feature of the American West that serves in cowboy verse as both a passage and a barrier, a place of danger and revelation."

memes.label: "Cattle Roping"
memes.description: "The quintessential cowboy skill—roping cattle from horseback with precision and grace, celebrated in verse as both practical art and competitive sport."

memes.label: "Cayuse"
memes.description: "A small, hardy horse of the American West—named for the Cayuse people of the Columbia Plateau—appearing in cattle-trail verse as the cowboy's mount, whether spirited and admired or starved and mourned on the desert. (032-001)(061-001)(075-001)"

memes.label: "Chaparral"
memes.description: "A dense thicket of shrubs and small trees—the rugged, thorny vegetation of the southwestern landscape that serves as both obstacle and refuge in cowboy verse."

memes.label: "Chaps"
memes.description: "Chaps—the leather leggings that protected the cowboy from brush, thorns, and rope burns—as both practical equipment and potent symbol of cowboy identity in the cattle trail poems."

memes.label: Charles Badger Clark
memes.description: American cowboy poet (1883–1957), the most prolific contributor to Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp, celebrated for "High Chin Bob" and other Western ballads that bridge folk tradition and literary poetry.

memes.label: Charles Pitt
memes.description: "Poet who contributed 'The Bandit's Grave' (ch065) to the Lomax anthology, a desert elegy for a border bandit buried beneath cactus and poppy."

memes.label: "Cheyenne"
memes.description: "Cheyenne appears in the poems as a wild trail town of faro layouts and rough living—a place where a cowboy's credentials were tested against the hardest the West could offer. Its name alone, in \"The Insult,\" serves as shorthand for frontier authenticity."

memes.label: Chihuahua
memes.description: Mexican state bordering Texas, referenced in cowboy poetry as a source of cross-border cattle and courier routes. (014-001)(027-001)

memes.label: "Chisholm Trail"
memes.description: "The Chisholm Trail—the most celebrated cattle trail of the era, running from Texas through Indian Territory to Kansas railheads—appears in a nostalgic toast to the round-up life, where punching drags on the old trail embodies a lost world of youth and freedom."

memes.label: "Cinch"
memes.description: "A double-edged cowboy term—both the saddle-girth that secures a rider's seat and, figuratively, a sure thing or guaranteed outcome—appearing in cattle-trail verse in both its literal and metaphorical senses. (026-001)(042-001)"

memes.label: "Clark and Adams: Two Poetic Visions of the West"
memes.description: "A comparative analysis of Charles Badger Clark and James Barton Adams — the anthology's two most prominent poets — exploring their complementary visions of the cowboy experience: Clark the moral geographer, Adams the social observer."

memes.label: "Colorado"
memes.description: "Colorado as a key region on the cattle trail—the Colorado River, the state's plains, and the cowpunchers who called it home."

memes.label: Index of Concepts
memes.description: A catalog of cowboy concepts, practices, traditions, and terminology — from the round-up to the stampede, from the saddle to the sombrero — as documented in Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp.

memes.label: "Corral"
memes.description: "A pen or enclosure for livestock—the center of cowboy work, a recurring symbol of home, courtship, and confrontation in the poems of Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp."

memes.label: "Courage and Bravery"
memes.description: "The cowboy's code of courage—facing stampedes, storms, and hostile encounters without flinching, as celebrated throughout the anthology."

memes.label: Cowboy Culture
memes.description: The culture, traditions, code of honor, and way of life of the American cowboy as depicted in the poetry of Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp — a world of cattle drives, bunkhouse ballads, and the unwritten laws of the trail.

memes.label: "Cowboy Humor"
memes.description: "Cowboy humor as a distinctive mode of Western storytelling — a blend of understatement, exaggeration, self-mockery, and tall-tale bravado that pervades the Lomax anthology's comic poems."

memes.label: "Coyote Imagery"
memes.description: "The coyote as a recurring auditory and symbolic image in the cattle trail poems—its howl the soundtrack of the night range, embodying both the wildness and the loneliness of the cowboy's world."

relations.label: *Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp*

memes.label: "Dakota"
memes.description: "Dakota represents the northernmost reach of the cattle trail—the 'distant sands' that marked the far boundary of the cowboy's world. In '[[A-Cowboy-Toast|A Cowboy Toast]],' it anchors the geographic sweep from the [[llano-estacado|Llano Estacado]] to the northern plains."

memes.label: "Danger and Peril"
memes.description: "The ever-present threat of stampede, storm, and violent encounter that defines the cowboy's relationship with mortality on the cattle trail."

memes.label: "Dawn Imagery"
memes.description: "Dawn as a transitional image in the cattle trail poems—representing hope, renewal, and the boundary between night's solitude and day's labor on the range."

memes.label: "Death and Mortality"
memes.description: "The theme of death on the cattle trail—stampede, gunfight, exposure, and the cowboy's intimate acquaintance with mortality."

memes.label: "Death Imagery"
memes.description: "Death as a recurring poetic image in the cattle-trail ballads — the ever-present risk of the trail, the violent end, and the stoic cowboy's confrontation with mortality."

memes.label: "Denver"
memes.description: "Denver as a trail town where cowboys let off steam—the site of dances, carnivals, and chance encounters in the cattle trail poems. Home to the outlaw character Denver Jim."

memes.label: Denver Jim
memes.description: "Outlaw protagonist of Sherman D. Richardson's frontier ballad, captured by vigilantes but spared when a letter from his mother reveals the human being behind the criminal."

memes.label: "Denver Jim (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the character study of Denver Jim, a figure of the trail whose story captures the loneliness and moral complexity of cowboy life."

memes.label: "Desert Imagery"
memes.description: "The desert as a defining landscape image in the cattle trail poems—a space of extremes that tests survival, strips away pretense, and reveals character through its unforgiving beauty."

memes.label: "Dodge City"
memes.description: "Dodge City, [[kansas]]—the legendary cowtown at the end of [[the-cattle-trail]]—appears in 'The Cattle Round-Up' as the destination toward which the trail-driving cowboy rode each year. Paired inseparably with the [[chisholm-trail]], it symbolizes release, celebration, and the memory of a lost golden age."

memes.label: "Dogie"
memes.description: "A motherless calf on the range—one of the most evocative terms in the cowboy lexicon—appearing in cattle-trail verse in the well-worn phrase about not 'crowding the [[dogie]] stars.' (009-001)"

memes.label: "Dust Imagery"
memes.description: "The omnipresent dust of the cattle trail—choking, blinding, yet also the badge of honest labor and the signature of the herd on the move."

memes.label: E. A. Brinninstool
memes.description: "American cowboy poet and historian (1870–1957) who contributed two poems to the Lomax anthology, specializing in Western Americana and cowboy life."

memes.label: "Fire Imagery"
memes.description: "Fire—especially the campfire—as the central image of warmth, community, and civilization in the cattle trail poems: the hearth of the range, where cowboys gather, sing, and reclaim their humanity after the day's labor."

memes.label: Frank Desprez
memes.description: "English-born poet (1853–1916), author of \"Lasca,\" the tragic borderland love poem included in John A. Lomax's *Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp*."

memes.label: "Freedom"
memes.description: "The cowboy's defining value—freedom from fences, freedom from bosses, and the open range as the last domain of American liberty."

memes.label: "Freedom of the Range"
memes.description: "The cowboy's conception of freedom as expressed through the unbounded Western landscape—a freedom both exhilarating and isolating, defined by open skies, untamed winds, and the absence of fences."

memes.label: "Friendship and Camaraderie"
memes.description: "The bonds of friendship forged on the trail—between cowboys, between cowboy and horse, and between strangers who become trail partners."

memes.label: "Gila River"
memes.description: "The Gila River, flowing through present-day Arizona and New Mexico, appears in two cowboy poems—as the setting for a round-up and a bronc-riding contest in \"[[The-Legend-of-Boastful-Bill|The Legend of Boastful Bill]],\" and as the namesake of the treacherous \"[[The-Gila-Monster-Route|Gila Monster Route]]\" where stranded men face the desert's emptiness."

memes.label: Glenn Norton
memes.description: "Co-author of 'The Gila Monster Route' (ch070) with L. F. Post, a narrative poem about a hobo stranded in the desert after being thrown off a freight train."

memes.label: Henry Herbert Knibbs
memes.description: "American cowboy poet (1874–1945), one of the two major literary contributors (alongside [[Charles-Badger-Clark|Charles Badger Clark]]) to John A. Lomax's anthology *Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp* (1916)."

memes.label: High Chin Bob
memes.description: "Proud cowboy protagonist of Charles Badger Clark's narrative ballad, who ropes a mountain lion in the Mokiones and refuses to let go, embodying the cowboy code of persistence against impossible odds."

memes.label: "High Chin Bob (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the darkly comic tale of High Chin Bob, a horse thief whose glib tongue cannot save him from frontier justice."

memes.label: "Home and Belonging"
memes.description: "The cowboy's longing for home—a place that exists more vividly in memory than in geography, representing stability, love, and belonging beyond the trail."

memes.label: "Homesickness"
memes.description: "The pervasive theme of longing for home—a specific ranch, a distant town, or an idealized domestic space—that runs through the cattle trail poems as the emotional counterpoint to the cowboy's nomadic life."

memes.label: "Horse Imagery"
memes.description: "The horse as the central animal image in cattle trail poetry—the cowboy's essential companion, measure of skill, and symbol of freedom and partnership on the open range."

memes.label: "Humor and Comedy"
memes.description: "The comic tradition in cowboy poetry—tall tales, practical jokes, self-deprecation, and the humor that made trail life bearable."

memes.label: "Idaho"
memes.description: "Idaho appears in \"The Legend of Boastful Bill\" as the origin of a notably unbreakable horse at a round-up on the Gila, evoking the region's reputation for tough stock in cowboy lore."

memes.label: "Identity and Self-Definition"
memes.description: "The cowboy's ongoing project of self-definition—what it means to be a cowboy, to belong to the West, and to live by an unwritten code."

memes.label: Index of Imagery
memes.description: A catalog of the recurring images and sensory patterns — horses, stars, fire, wind, sage, rivers — that form the poetic vocabulary of Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp.

memes.label: Jack Lorell
memes.description: "Character in \"The Ballad of Billy Leamont,\" a tragic cowboy ballad fragment preserved in Chapter 74. Jack Lorell rides at Billy Leamont's shoulder as they leave town, but is found dead in a dell — the event that drives Leamont to suicide. (074-001)"

relations.label: Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp

memes.label: James Barton Adams
memes.description: American cowboy poet and major contributor to Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp (1916), known for humorous and sentimental verses that capture the cowboy's social life — dances, campfires, and bunkhouse camaraderie.

memes.label: Jim Kershaw
memes.description: "Professional gambler who appears in 'The Dance at Silver Valley' (ch005), where his rivalry with Broncho Bill over Belle McClure sets up a violent confrontation."

memes.label: John A. Lomax
memes.description: "American folklorist, musicologist, and editor (1867–1948) who collected and published *Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp* (1916) — the founding document of cowboy song preservation and the primary source for this wiki."

relations.label: Cowboy Songs and Other Frontier Ballads

memes.label: "Kansas"
memes.description: "Kansas as a destination of the cattle trail—the railhead at Kansas City, the homesteading townships, and the bluffs that shaped cowboy life."

memes.label: L. F. Post
memes.description: "Co-author of 'The Gila Monster Route' (ch070) with Glenn Norton, a narrative poem about a hobo 'ditched' by a freight train in the desert."

memes.label: L. Worthington Green
memes.description: "Poet who contributed 'The Bull Fight' (ch027) to the Lomax anthology, a dramatic narrative of a Mexican corrida culminating in the death of the bull."

memes.label: "Laramie"
memes.description: "Laramie, Wyoming, appears in \"Snagtooth Sal\" as the Wyoming town where the young cowboy narrator walks happily with his sweetheart—a memory of lost love set against the backdrop of a frontier cattle town on the Union Pacific line."

memes.label: Larry Chittenden
memes.description: "American cowboy poet (1862–1934), author of 'The Cowboys' Christmas Ball' — one of the best-known cowboy poems in the Lomax anthology, so widely adopted by oral tradition that cowboys sang it as their own."

memes.label: Lasca
memes.description: "Lasca — the passionate Mexican heroine of Frank Desprez's narrative poem in the Lomax anthology, a wild, free-spirited vaquera who rides alongside the narrator in love and danger, and dies saving him from a cattle stampede."

memes.label: "Lasca (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of 'Lasca' by Frank Desprez — the anthology's longest and most ambitious narrative poem, a tragic romance of love and death on the Texas range told in galloping anapestic meter."

memes.label: Lasso
memes.description: "The cowboy's rope with a running noose, used for catching cattle and horses on the trail—a defining tool of the working cowboy in the anthology (063-001)."

memes.label: "Latigo"
memes.description: "The leather strap that fastens the cinch to the saddle—a small but vital piece of cowboy gear whose creak and strain mark the rhythm of the ride in cattle-trail verse. (060-001)"

memes.label: "Llano Estacado"
memes.description: "The Llano Estacado, or Staked Plains—a vast, arid plateau spanning eastern New Mexico and northwestern Texas—appears in two poems as a symbol of the cowboy's harsh and unforgiving range, marking both a trail's dead-end and the southern boundary of the cowboy's remembered world."

memes.label: "Loneliness"
memes.description: "The defining emotional condition of the cattle trail cowboy—a loneliness born of vast spaces, long absences, and the irreducible solitude of the night guard, expressed as both burden and badge of identity."

memes.label: "Loss and Mortality"
memes.description: "Loss—of life, love, and the open range itself—as a shadow theme running through the cattle trail poems, surfacing in elegies for fallen comrades and in the elegiac awareness that the cowboy's world is already passing."

memes.label: "Love and Courtship"
memes.description: "Romantic love as a recurring counterpoint to the cattle trail's harshness—the woman left behind, the dance-hall courtship, and the beloved whose memory sustains the cowboy through his solitary labors."

memes.label: Mackenzie Trail
memes.description: "A historic cattle trail and wagon route across Texas, celebrated in John A. Lomax's poem \"The Old Mackenzie Trail\" (Chapter 66). The trail crossed the Brazos, Colorado, and Concho rivers, passed by Abilene onto the Llano Estacado, and was named for the frontier military commander Ranald S. Mackenzie. (066-001)"

memes.label: Margaret Ashmun
memes.description: "Poet who contributed 'The Vigilantes' (ch064) to the Lomax anthology, a dramatic poem celebrating frontier vigilante justice as a righteous force."

memes.label: Marta of Milrone
memes.description: "The cowgirl companion of the narrator in the ballad \"Marta of Milrone\" (ch020), a figure of freedom and companionship on the cattle trails of the desert Southwest."

memes.label: "Maverick"
memes.description: "An unbranded calf or yearling—and by extension, a person who stands outside the herd, a figure of independence and wildness in cowboy lore and verse."

memes.label: "Mesa"
memes.description: "A flat-topped elevation rising from the surrounding plain—a distinctive landmark of the southwestern landscape that appears in cowboy verse as a vantage point and a symbol of the open country. (032-001)(073-001)"

memes.label: "Mesquite"
memes.description: "A hardy desert tree of the American Southwest—a landmark of the cattle trail whose gnarled forms serve as witnesses to gunfights, loneliness, and the passage of time in cowboy poetry."

memes.label: "Missouri"
memes.description: "Missouri appears in the poems as a touchstone of exaggeration—a state whose native sons would recognize the scale of cowboy boasting, anchoring Western tall tales in a familiar American reference point."

memes.label: "Mokiones"
memes.description: "The Mokiones are the mountain setting of Charles Badger Clark's \"High Chin Bob,\" a high-country landscape where a lion kills a yearling and the boastful cowboy hero attempts an audacious rope catch."

memes.label: "Montana"
memes.description: "Montana as the northern terminus of the cattle trail—the destination toward which the long drives moved, representing both the end of the trail and the promise of open grazing land."

memes.label: "Moon Imagery"
memes.description: "The moon as a recurrent celestial image in the cattle trail poems—a silent witness to cowboy life that illuminates nocturnal scenes of reflection, romance, and solitude on the open range."

memes.label: "Mountain Imagery"
memes.description: "Mountains as images of permanence, distance, and aspiration in the cattle trail poems—the horizon's backbone, marking the edge of the cowboy's known world with snow-capped certainty."

memes.label: "Music and Song"
memes.description: "Music as an ever-present force in cowboy life—from the cattle trail songs that paced the long drives to the fiddle tunes that set frontier dances spinning, as depicted throughout the anthology."

memes.label: "Mustang"
memes.description: "The wild horse of the American West—descended from Spanish stock and running free across the plains—appearing in cattle-trail verse as the cowboy's preferred mount, a 'mouse-grey' companion on the trail. (010-001)"

memes.label: "Nature and the Natural World"
memes.description: "The vast, indifferent, and beautiful natural world of the cattle trail—prairie, mountain, river, and sky as both adversary and companion."

memes.label: "New Mexico"
memes.description: "New Mexico appears in the Introduction as a place where cowboy songs were sung and collected—a territory of the cattle trail era that shaped Western verse."

memes.label: "Night Imagery"
memes.description: "The recurring imagery of night, darkness, and moonlight across the cattle trail poems, serving as a backdrop for solitude, danger, and the cowboy's vigil."

memes.label: "Oregon"
memes.description: "Oregon appears in \"Jack Dempsey's Grave\" as the remote Western wilderness where the famed boxer Jack Dempsey lies buried—far from the Eastern cities that once acclaimed him, amid the 'giant fir and cedar' of the Pacific Northwest."

memes.label: Out Where the West Begins
memes.description: Literary analysis of "Out Where the West Begins," Charles Badger Clark's opening poem in Part I of the Lomax anthology — a rhetorical masterpiece defining Western identity through anaphora, landscape, and the cowboy's moral geography.

memes.label: "Outlaw"
memes.description: "In the language of the cattle trail, an outlaw is both a man outside the law and a horse or steer that refuses to be broken—a figure of dangerous independence that haunts the borderland between civilization and the wild."

memes.label: "Pecos"
memes.description: "The Pecos River and its surrounding country as a key geographic marker in the cattle trail poems—a landmark on the long drive from Texas to the northern ranges."

memes.label: Index of Places
memes.description: A complete index of all places — trails, rivers, towns, regions, and landmarks — named in the poems of Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp.

memes.label: Pocock
memes.description: "Poet known for the dialect verse narrative 'Curley,' quoted in four poems across the Lomax anthology as an epigraph source."

memes.label: Index of Poets
memes.description: A complete index of all poets and authors represented in Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp, with their quality ratings and key works.

memes.label: "Powder River"
memes.description: "The Powder River in Wyoming and Montana appears in two cattle-trail poems—'The Legend of Boastful Bill' and 'The Bronc That Wouldn't Bust'—as a geographical marker of the northern range and the place where legendary broncos tested the courage of cowboys."

memes.label: "Prairie"
memes.description: "The vast grassland of the American West—the open range that defines the cowboy's domain, a landscape of freedom, loneliness, and the long horizon that shapes the verse of the cattle trail."

memes.label: "Prairie Imagery"
memes.description: "The prairie as the fundamental landscape image of the cattle trail poems—the vast, grass-covered expanse that is simultaneously the cowboy's workplace, home, and spiritual territory."

memes.label: "Puncher"
memes.description: "A cowboy—from 'cowpuncher,' a term rooted in the working vocabulary of the range. In the verse of the cattle trail, the puncher is the everyman of the open country: skilled, proud, and at home in a world defined by horses, cattle, and the trail."

memes.label: "Quirt"
memes.description: "A short, braided leather riding whip with a looped handle—an essential piece of the cowboy's gear, used to urge a horse forward and, in cattle-trail verse, a symbol of motion, mastery, and the sudden violence of the range. (006-001)(008-001)(035-001)(040-001)(073-001)"

memes.label: "Rain Imagery"
memes.description: "Rain and storm as images of hardship, danger, and the indifferent power of nature in the cattle trail poems—the weather that tests the cowboy's resilience and defines the range's harsh beauty."

memes.label: "Riders Imagery"
memes.description: "The figure of the rider — rough riders, bow-legged boys, star-dusted horsemen — as the anthology's central heroic image of the cowboy."

memes.label: "Riders of the Stars (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of 'Riders of the Stars' by Henry Herbert Knibbs — a visionary meditation on the cowboy as cosmic rider, transforming the physical labor of trail driving into a spiritual journey among the constellations."

memes.label: "Riding Imagery"
memes.description: "The act of riding as central poetic image — the cowboy's defining motion, the horse as companion, and the ceaseless movement across the range that defines trail life."

memes.label: Rio Grande
memes.description: The great river of the American Southwest, forming the Texas-Mexico border — a recurring geographical and symbolic landmark in cowboy poetry, marking both the edge of the cattle kingdom and the meeting of Anglo and Mexican vaquero cultures.

memes.label: "River Imagery"
memes.description: "Rivers as recurring imagery in the anthology—the Rio Grande, the Brazos, the Pecos—waterways as boundaries, obstacles, and symbols of the journey north."

memes.label: "Rocky Mountains"
memes.description: "The Rocky Mountains appear in James Fox's 'Where the Grizzly Dwells' as the spiritual heart of the American West—a landscape of 'virginal beauty' where the cowboy finds freedom, solitude, and a connection to the eternal amid foothills, timber, and high peaks."

memes.label: "Round-Up"
memes.description: "The seasonal gathering of scattered cattle across the open range—the central event of the cowboy's working year and a recurring setting in cattle-trail poetry, evoking camaraderie, hard riding, and the rituals of range life. (006-001)(008-001)(009-001)(023-001)(033-001)"

memes.label: "Rustler"
memes.description: "A cattle thief of the open range—an outlaw figure who haunts the borderland between law and lawlessness—appearing in cattle-trail verse as one face of the frontier's moral ambiguity. (004-001)"

memes.label: "Sage Imagery"
memes.description: "Sage as the defining botanical image of the Western range in the cattle trail poems—the plant whose scent, color, and ubiquity make it the olfactory signature of the cowboy's world."

memes.label: "San Antonio"
memes.description: "San Antonio appears in [[frank-desprez]]'s '[[lasca]]' as the [[texas]] town where the narrator's flirtation with 'a glorious girl in the [[alamo]]' provokes a jealous, near-fatal dagger strike from his beloved [[lasca]]—a moment of passion in the heart of the cattle-trail South."

memes.label: "Santa Fe"
memes.description: "Santa Fe, the historic terminus of the Santa Fe Trail in New Mexico, appears in the collection as the route by which a strange traveler arrives at the Pecos camp—the comic premise of 'Broncho Versus Bicycle,' one of the anthology's most sustained humorous narratives."

memes.label: "Singing and Songmaking"
memes.description: "The theme of music-making as the cowboy's primary creative and emotional outlet—a refuge from isolation, a tool for calming cattle, and a means of preserving experience through oral tradition in the cattle trail poems."

memes.label: "Sky Imagery"
memes.description: "The immense Western sky as a dominant visual motif in the cattle trail poems—at once canvas, companion, and measure of the cowboy's world, appearing in 17 passages across the anthology."

memes.label: Snagtooth Sal
memes.description: "Snagtooth Sal — the tragic-comic heroine of the cowboy lament in the Lomax anthology, a Laramie girl whose memory haunts the narrator of one of the collection's most poignant relationship ballads."

memes.label: "Snagtooth Sal (Analysis)"
memes.description: Literary analysis of "Snagtooth Sal," Lowell O. Reese's comic-elegiac cowboy love poem — a humorous yet mournful ballad about a cowboy who loses his sweetheart, blending Western vernacular with the tradition of the comic lament.

memes.label: "Snow Imagery"
memes.description: "Snow as an image of seasonal change, purity, and distance in the cattle trail poems—the white on distant peaks that marks winter and contrasts with the warm lowlands of the [[cowboy-culture|cowboy]]'s working world."

memes.label: "Sombrero"
memes.description: "The wide-brimmed hat of the southwestern cowboy—more than protection from sun and weather, a symbol of identity, pride, and the borderland fusion that defines [[cowboy-culture]] in verse."

memes.label: "Songs of the Cattle Trail (Title Poem Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analytical page for the anthology's title poem, examining how 'Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp' establishes the collection's governing themes: movement, music, and the cowboy's relationship to the Western landscape."

memes.label: Sonora
memes.description: Mexican state bordering Arizona, mentioned in cowboy poetry as cattle country south of the U.S. border. (014-001)(024-001)

memes.label: "Spurs"
memes.description: "The cowboy's spurs—tool, adornment, and status symbol, their jingle as characteristic of the trail as the song of the night guard."

memes.label: "Stampede"
memes.description: "The stampede as the most feared event on the cattle trail — a sudden mass panic that could scatter a herd across miles of prairie, destroy weeks of work, and kill men and horses caught in its path."

memes.label: "Stars Imagery"
memes.description: "The stars as the cowboy's celestial companions—guides for night herding, witnesses to solitude, and symbols of the vast Western sky in the cattle trail poems."

memes.label: "Texas"
memes.description: "Texas as the starting point and origin of the cattle trail, home to many cowboys whose songs and stories fill this anthology."

memes.label: "Texas and Kansas: Origin and Destination"
memes.description: "A comparative analysis of how Texas and Kansas function as symbolic opposites in the anthology's emotional geography — Texas as the place of origin, identity, and belonging; Kansas as the place of arrival, commerce, and transition."
aliases: origin and destination in cowboy poetry

memes.label: "Texas Tom"
memes.description: "Texas Tom, a recurring character and archetype of the storytelling cowboy in the anthology—a figure whose yarns, courtships, and adventures represent the oral tradition and companionable humor of trail life."

memes.label: "The Bunkhouse Orchestra (Analysis)"
memes.description: Literary analysis of "The Bunkhouse Orchestra," an anonymous cowboy poem celebrating music-making in the bunkhouse — a vivid portrait of the cowboy's domestic life on winter nights, where mouth-harps and banjos transform the hardships of the trail into communal joy.

memes.label: The Cattle Trail
memes.description: The great cattle trail system of the American West — from Texas to Montana, from the Rio Grande to the northern railheads — the geographical and experiential backbone of cowboy poetry in the Lomax anthology.

memes.label: The Cattleman
memes.description: "The cattleman — rancher, trail boss, and industry architect — as depicted in cowboy poetry: the employer, elder, and symbol of an era when \"the prairie was big and the cattle\" defined the American West."

memes.label: "The Chuck Wagon"
memes.description: "The chuck wagon as the mobile kitchen and social hub of the trail drive — the wagon that fed the crew, anchored the camp, and cultivated the cook whose authority on the range rivaled the trail boss's."

memes.label: "The Trail Cook"
memes.description: "The cook was the unchallenged monarch of the chuck wagon—dispenser of food, medicine, and morale, and often the oldest and most respected hand on the drive."

memes.label: The Cowboy and His Song
memes.description: A synthetic analysis of the cowboy song tradition as preserved in the Lomax anthology — the oral poetry of the trail that transformed labor into art, from anonymous trail ballads to the literary compositions of Clark and Adams.

relations.label: Cowboy Songs and Other Frontier Ballads

memes.label: "The Cowboy and His Song (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analytical companion to the featured page on 'The Cowboy and His Song,' examining the poem's role as a meta-commentary on the anthology's own project—the cowboy's relationship to music-making and oral tradition."

memes.label: "The Cowboy Code"
memes.description: "The unwritten ethical code governing cowboy behavior — an informal system of honor, loyalty, courage, and hospitality that poets in the anthology both practiced and celebrated."

memes.label: "The Cowboys' Christmas Ball (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of 'The Cowboys' Christmas Ball' — the anthology's definitive festive poem, immortalizing a real dance held in Anson City, Texas, in 1885, and capturing the collision of frontier roughness with holiday celebration."

memes.label: "The Cowboy's Dance Song (Analysis)"
memes.description: Literary analysis of "The Cowboy's Dance Song," an anonymous poem capturing the comic collision between cowboy culture and "high-toned" society — a fish-out-of-water narrative in which a cowboy at a Denver dance becomes both the butt of the joke and its most sympathetic figure.

memes.label: "The Cowboy's Valentine (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the tender poem in which a cowboy sends his heart across the miles to his distant beloved—love and longing on the cattle trail."

memes.label: "The End of the Trail (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of 'The End of the Trail' — the anthology's closing poem, a meditation on mortality, memory, and what remains when the cowboy's journey is done."

memes.label: "The Glory Trail (Analysis)"
memes.description: Literary analysis of "The Glory Trail," Charles Badger Clark's rollicking narrative poem about High-Chin Bob's mountain lion hunt — a comic masterpiece of cowboy bravado that transforms a routine predator kill into a mock-heroic quest for "the glory trail."

memes.label: "The Legend of Boastful Bill (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the tall tale of Boastful Bill, whose bragging meets its match in a bucking bronco—a comic exploration of pride, humiliation, and cowboy humor."

memes.label: "The Long Drive"
memes.description: "The epic cattle drive from Texas ranges to northern railheads—the defining labor of the cowboy era, spanning months and hundreds of miles."

memes.label: "The Maverick"
memes.description: "Unbranded cattle roaming the open range—the maverick represented both opportunity (free wealth for the taking) and the lawless spirit of the unfenced West."

memes.label: "The Night Guard"
memes.description: "The cowboy's solitary night watch over the herd—a ritual of vigilance, song, and communion with the dark prairie."

memes.label: "The Old Chisholm Trail (Analysis)"
memes.description: "Analysis of the classic trail-driving song celebrating the Chisholm Trail—the cowboy's humorous catalog of hardships, wages, and the lure of the open range."

memes.label: "The Remuda"
memes.description: "The herd of spare horses that accompanied every cattle drive—each cowboy required multiple mounts, making the remuda essential trail infrastructure."

memes.label: "The Round-Up"
memes.description: "The round-up as the central organizing event of cattle trail life—the seasonal gathering of cattle across vast ranges that defined the cowboy's annual rhythm and tested his skills at their peak."

memes.label: "The Saddle"
memes.description: "The saddle as the cowboy's most essential tool — not mere equipment but the seat of his working identity, appearing throughout the anthology as a symbol of the rider's bond with his horse and his craft."

memes.label: "The Sombrero"
memes.description: "The sombrero as the cowboy's most visible symbol—the wide-brimmed hat that shields from sun and rain, marks its wearer's identity, and appears throughout the cattle trail poems as shorthand for the cowboy himself."

memes.label: "The Trail Boss"
memes.description: "The trail boss—commander of the long drive, responsible for the herd, the crew, and the success of the months-long journey from Texas to the northern railheads."

memes.label: "The West"
memes.description: "The West as both geographic reality and mythic construct in the cattle trail poems—a place defined by its vastness, its freedom, and its transitional moment between the open range and settlement."

memes.label: "The Wrangler"
memes.description: "The cowboy responsible for the remuda—often the youngest hand on the drive, the wrangler's skill with horses was essential to trail operations."

memes.label: Index of Themes
memes.description: A catalog of the major themes — loneliness, camaraderie, the vanishing frontier, nature, work, death — that run through the poetry of Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp.

memes.label: "Trail Driving"
memes.description: "Trail driving — the multi-month journey of moving cattle from Texas ranches to northern railheads — as the defining labor of the cowboy era, celebrated throughout the anthology's poems."

memes.label: "Water Imagery"
memes.description: "Water as an image of life, danger, and transformation in the cattle trail poems—the rivers that must be crossed, the waterholes that sustain, and the thirst that defines the arid range."

memes.label: Whiskey Bill
memes.description: "A wild, gun-toting cowboy character in the anonymous poem \"Whiskey Bill—A Fragment,\" whose drunken sprees terrorize the town until a fatal confrontation with the sheriff. (062-001)"

memes.label: "Wichita"
memes.description: "Wichita, Kansas, appears in \"To Hear Him Tell It\" as a cattle trail destination where cowboys delivered herds across the Kansas line in the early days of the trail-driving era."

memes.label: William Maxwell
memes.description: "American cowboy poet, author of \"[[The-Dance-at-Silver-Valley|The Dance at Silver Valley]]\" (also known as \"[[broncho-bill|Broncho Bill]] and Belle McClure\"), a narrative poem of jealousy and violence in John A. Lomax's *Songs of the Cattle Trail and Cow Camp*."

memes.label: "Wind Imagery"
memes.description: "The wind as a constant presence in the cattle trail poems—carrying sound, shaping landscape, and embodying the untamed freedom of the open range."

memes.label: "Work and Labor"
memes.description: "The cowboy's work—herding, roping, branding, night-guarding—as the defining activity of the cattle trail poems, treated not as backdrop but as the substance of identity and the measure of a man."
